message:
Update beads tracking: close repair parallelization, open fsck --force

- Close bd-21m0 (repair --max-threads scrub parallelization) as completed
- Create bd-2ahn (implement real fsck --force semantics) as new in-progress task
  to make fsck --force meaningful by skipping full ext4 scrub when filesystem
  appears clean unless --force is set

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1yqb","title":"FUSE: bound AccessPredictor state to prevent unbounded growth","description":"AccessPredictor currently grows key maps without eviction, causing unbounded memory growth on long-running mounts with high inode churn. Add bounded retention/eviction and tests proving map size remains capped while preserving recency behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-19T02:21:48.827415369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T02:34:08.046242564Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T02:34:08.046224270Z","close_reason":"Completed: AccessPredictor now bounded with LRU-style eviction; added cap/eviction tests; rch gates clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","fuse","memory","predictor"]}

Ran fmt/check/clippy/test successfully.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:54:05.875288741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:26.320857160Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:26.320763184Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-20p","title":"Implement multi-threaded FUSE dispatch with Send+Sync state","description":"# Implement multi-threaded FUSE dispatch with Send+Sync state

## GOAL
Enable concurrent FUSE request handling using fuser multi-threaded mode, as mandated by spec §1.7 Mechanical Sympathy.

## BACKGROUND
The spec states: "MUST use multi-threaded dispatch. All `Filesystem` state MUST be `Send + Sync`. `parking_lot` locks for concurrency."

Single-threaded FUSE dispatch is a severe bottleneck:
- 5-10μs per kernel/userspace round-trip
- Sequential ops block on I/O
- CPU underutilization on multi-core

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. FuseBackend State Architecture
```rust
pub struct FuseBackend {
    // Arc-wrapped for multi-threaded access
    inner: Arc<FuseInner>,
}

struct FuseInner {
    fs: RwLock<OpenFs>,           // parking_lot RwLock
    cache: Arc<ArcCache>,          // Already Send+Sync
    mvcc: Arc<MvccStore>,          // Already Send+Sync
    config: MountConfig,           // Immutable after mount
    metrics: AtomicMetrics,        // Lock-free counters
}
```

### 2. Thread Pool Configuration
- Default: min(num_cpus, 8) threads
- Configurable via MountConfig
- Each thread has its own I/O budget tracking

### 3. Lock Granularity
- ArcCache: Internal fine-grained locking (already done)
- MvccStore: Per-block version chain locks
- OpenFs: Per-operation RwLock (readers concurrent, writers exclusive)
- Metrics: Atomic counters (no locks)

### 4. Avoid False Sharing
- Align hot counters to cache line (64 bytes)
- Separate read-heavy and write-heavy state

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit test: FuseBackend is Send + Sync (compile-time)
2. Integration: 100 concurrent read requests complete without deadlock
3. Integration: 10 concurrent write + 90 concurrent read complete
4. Stress: 10 threads x 1000 ops each, verify no data corruption

## LOGGING
- Log thread pool initialization (thread count)
- Log per-thread request dispatch (trace level)
- Log lock contention events (warn level)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `cargo test` confirms FuseBackend: Send + Sync
2. Concurrent benchmark shows >4x throughput vs single-threaded
3. No deadlocks in 1-hour stress test
4. Metrics show balanced load across threads","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:19.657268383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T10:05:04.975125973Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T10:05:04.975035524Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","core","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.189131817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.462340437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-3cjj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.024839399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-22w","title":"EPIC: MVCC Core Completion","description":"# EPIC: MVCC Core Completion

## PURPOSE
Complete the MVCC (Multi-Version Concurrency Control) subsystem to production quality. Currently at 28.6% (4/14).

## BACKGROUND
MVCC is FrankenFS's key architectural innovation over kernel ext4/btrfs. It enables:
- Concurrent readers + writers without JBD2's global lock
- Snapshot isolation for consistent reads
- First-committer-wins (FCW) and SSI conflict detection

From FEATURE_PARITY.md:
- MVCC snapshot visibility: ✅
- MVCC commit sequencing: ✅
- FCW conflict detection: ✅
- Version retention policy: ✅ (in-memory)
- COW block rewrite path: 🟡 PARTIAL (basic version copy only)

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. MVCC persistence - version chains survive restart (WAL)
2. Version chain compression - reduce memory overhead
3. GC optimization - efficient pruning under active snapshots
4. COW block rewrite path - complete write-side semantics

## ALIEN-ARTIFACT QUALITY BAR (from AGENTS.md)
MVCC conflict logic is explicitly called out for principled treatment:
- explicit invariants
- evidence ledger for commit/reject decisions
- formal SSI cycle detection (or equivalent correctness argument + tests)

## CURRENT STATE
- `MvccStore` with in-memory version chains
- Transaction APIs (`begin` / `commit` / `commit_ssi`)
- Snapshot visibility via search
- GC via `prune_versions_older_than` and watermarking

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. MVCC persistence layer (WAL or equivalent)
2. Version chain memory bounded (compression and/or GC policy)
3. Memory overhead documented and benchmarked
4. Conflict detection formally verified (proptest + deterministic interleavings)
5. MVCC/COW reaches 70%+ parity (10/14)

## DEPENDENCIES
- EPIC: Conformance & Quality Infrastructure (bd-2jk) - need baselines and goldens for safe iteration

## RELATED SPEC SECTIONS
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §3 (MVCC)
- PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md (ffs-mvcc crate)

## Success Criteria
1. Crash-restart tests demonstrate committed versions persist and uncommitted versions do not.
2. Deterministic concurrency tests (lab runtime) cover snapshot stability and FCW/SSI invariants.
3. Compression reduces memory on representative workloads and is covered by unit tests + microbenchmarks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:05.830436981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:46:04.233529434Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:46:04.233510488Z","close_reason":"All children closed. Cluster: concurrency_perf
Mapped graveyard sections: 14.10 EBR + 15.1 S3-FIFO + 15.4 Parallel WAL

Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:22Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-29z.2","title":"Add unit tests for btrfs write path: extent allocation, delayed refs, transactions","description":"# Add unit tests for btrfs write path: extent allocation, delayed refs, transactions

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the btrfs Write Path epic (bd-29z). Currently has E2E test (bd-2ju) but the only unit test is the standalone bd-3iyg which is blocked on bd-tbn and bd-3sj. This bead provides EARLY unit tests for the extent allocation layer that can run before the full transaction model is ready.

## TEST PLAN

### Extent Allocation Tests
1. Allocate extent: returns valid block range
2. Free extent: blocks returned to free space
3. Double-free detected: error returned
4. Allocate when full: ENOSPC returned
5. Allocation respects block group boundaries

### COW Tests
6. COW write: original block preserved, new block allocated
7. COW chain: multiple COW writes produce version chain
8. COW with MVCC: different transactions see different versions

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 8 tests above. Deterministic, mock block device.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log block ranges, free space state

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 8 tests pass
2. Tests runnable before transaction model is complete
3.  {"id":"bd-2a66","title":"Update PLAN_TO_PORT markers for completed items","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T16:05:49.965748248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:06:11.536645635Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T16:06:11.536627271Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2ad","title":"Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)","description":"# Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)

## GOAL
Implement robust mount lifecycle management with clean startup, shutdown, and signal handling.

## BACKGROUND
Production filesystems must handle:
- Clean mount with validation
- Graceful unmount with flush
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT)
- Crash recovery on restart

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Phase
```rust
pub async fn mount(config: MountConfig, cx: &Cx) -> Result<MountHandle> {
    // 1. Validate image (superblock, journal state)
    // 2. Replay journal if needed (ext4)
    // 3. Initialize MVCC store
    // 4. Initialize ARC cache
    // 5. Initialize repair manager
    // 6. Start background tasks (scrub, GC)
    // 7. Mount FUSE session
    // 8. Return handle for lifecycle control
}
```

### 2. Unmount Phase
```rust
impl MountHandle {
    pub async fn unmount(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<()> {
        // 1. Stop accepting new requests
        // 2. Wait for in-flight requests (with timeout)
        // 3. Flush dirty cache entries
        // 4. Commit pending transactions
        // 5. Refresh repair symbols for dirty groups
        // 6. Close FUSE session
        // 7. Sync and close device
    }
}
```

### 3. Signal Handling
- SIGTERM/SIGINT: Trigger graceful unmount
- SIGUSR1: Dump debug state to log
- Timeout for unmount (default 30s, then force)

### 4. Drop Safety
- MountHandle::drop must trigger unmount
- Panic in unmount must not corrupt filesystem
- Leaked handle must eventually timeout

## TESTS
1. Unit: Mount/unmount roundtrip
2. Integration: SIGTERM during I/O results in clean state
3. Integration: Double-mount same image fails cleanly
4. Stress: Mount/unmount loop 100x without leaks

## LOGGING
- Mount: log config, image path, mount point
- Unmount: log flush stats, pending ops
- Signal: log signal received, action taken

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount ... && ffs umount ...` leaves clean state
2. `kill -TERM $(pidof ffs)` results in clean unmount
3. Remount after crash shows no corruption
4. No resource leaks (file descriptors, memory)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:34.171965858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310382109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310314994Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","lifecycle","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:06.032850244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.263209229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-2ad","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This bead covers the 'FUSE mount runtime' row (currently ❌). When complete, flip to ✅ and update ParityReport::current() counts.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2bu","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement path lookup (linear scan) for name -> inode","description":"Goal: resolve paths like /a/b/c by walking directory inodes.

Deliverables:
- ffs-dir: lookup(parent_inode, name) -> inode_no.
- Use linear scan over parsed dir entries first (htree phased).
- Handle special entries: "." and "..".

Acceptance:
- Fixture test resolves a few known paths.
- Errors are correct: NotFound vs NotDirectory vs NameTooLong.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:26:01.193715842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:42:21.801329805Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:42:21.801311261Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bu","depends_on_id":"bd-2hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:33.976312271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2c1y","title":"MVCC durability: persist WAL before exposing commit","description":"Context: current PersistentMvccStore commit flow applies to in-memory store before WAL append, creating crash window where acknowledged commit is lost on replay.\n\nScope:\n- Reorder commit pipeline so WAL append/sync succeeds before in-memory visibility of commit.\n- Ensure WAL append failure returns error and does not expose transaction versions.\n- Add regression tests for WAL failure path and replay durability.\n\nAcceptance:\n- commit() does not mutate visible store state when WAL append fails.\n- successful commit remains replayable after restart.\n- targeted tests pass via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-19T02:07:05.755076103Z","created_by":"SilverLake","updated_at":"2026-02-19T02:21:03.539117230Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T02:21:03.539092574Z","close_reason":"Completed: WAL failure now rolls back in-memory commit state; mvcc durability tests and workspace gates pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","durability","mvcc","wal"]}
 {"id":"bd-2cn","title":"ext4: Expand superblock parsing toward full layout (ffs-ondisk)","description":"Goal: parse the ext4 superblock fields needed for mount validation and downstream math (group desc locations, inode table, features, checksums).

Deliverables (incremental):
- Parse additional core fields: s_log_cluster_size, s_blocks_per_group, s_inodes_per_group, s_inode_size, s_desc_size, s_feature_* full, s_uuid, s_checksum_seed (if present), s_checksum.
- Provide a structured representation for feature flags (bitflags or typed wrapper).
- Add tests that compare parsed values against known bytes (fixtures).

Acceptance:
- No panics on malformed data (ParseError only).
- validate_v1() can be implemented purely from parsed fields.
